dependencies: Significant portion of products manufactured or purchased outside of the US, primarily in Asia
Purchases from two wholesale distributors, Ingram Micro and TD SYNNEX, represent over 25% of total purchases
Significant portion of sales derived from products manufactured by Apple, Cisco, Dell Technologies, HP Inc., Lenovo and Microsoft
risks: Manufacturing interruptions or delays due to financial instability or bankruptcy of manufacturers
Significant labor disputes such as strikes
Product constraints due to failure of suppliers to accurately forecast customer demand
Supply chain disruptions causing volatility in inventory levels and delays in completion of orders
Concentration risk with key vendor partners and wholesale distributors

regulatory_risks: Noncompliance with contract provisions and government procurement regulations
Medicare and Medicaid Anti-Kickback Statute violations
Security clearance and confidentiality requirements violations
Suspension, debarment or ineligibility from doing business with governmental entities
Adoption of new or modified procurement regulations
New EU regulations on data privacy (GDPR)
Environmental, social and governance related laws and regulations
Imposition of additional duties, tariffs and other charges on imports and exports
FDA approval requirements (not explicitly mentioned but implied in healthcare segment)
Evolving laws and regulations during change in federal administration

sanction_risks: New or additional trade restrictions or sanctions imposed by US or foreign governments
Periods of intense diplomatic or armed conflict may result in new and rapidly evolving trade restrictions and sanctions
Embargoes, sanctions, safeguards and customs restrictions against products
Export regulations and sanctions compliance risks
